Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods 4.1 states gln2 is SALK_051953, At5g35630.

Source_Species.reason: The study uses Arabidopsis; Methods 4.1 states Arabidopsis thaliana WT and mutants.

Applied_Species.reason: The experiments were performed in Arabidopsis plants.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly measure 15N incorporation into glutamine and glutamate and monitor chloroplastic glutathione redox potential/chl-EGSH via chl-roGFP2.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Glutamine and glutamate are amino acids; chl-EGSH is a redox state/metabolite-related redox parameter. This classification is consistent with measured outputs.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results 2.2, 2.3, and 2.4 show gln2 has reduced 15N nitrate assimilation into Gln/Glu, lower ΦPSII under high light, and impaired chl-roGFP2 relaxation under high light.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/Methods: gln2 mutant analysis, immunoblot (Fig.1B), 15N nitrate LC-HRMS (Fig.2), chlorophyll fluorescence ΦPSII (Fig.3), chloroplast roGFP2 imaging (Figs.4-5), and elevated CO2 tests (Fig.6).

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered statement matches Results: reduced GS2 protein, lower 15N incorporation, reduced HL stimulation versus WT, stronger PSII photoinhibition, and impaired redox relaxation in gln2.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper identifies GS2 as glutamine synthetase 2; chloroplastic localization is implied by its role and naming in the paper.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: GS2 is discussed as part of the chloroplastic GS/GOGAT cycle and contrasted with cytosolic GS in Discussion, supporting chloroplast localization.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The study used whole plants/leaves of 2-3 week old plants under different light regimes, but it did not directly report a gene expression pattern for GS2.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These treatments are explicitly used: normal light 120, medium light 800, high light 1200, dark, and elevated CO2 700/900 ppm.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaf tissue was sampled for amino acid analysis and chl-roGFP2 reports chloroplast redox state, but the paper does not explicitly state glutamine/glutamate accumulation site as chloroplast.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Results/Abstract support that nitrogen assimilation products and chl-EGSH dynamics are linked to redox homeostasis and photoprotection under high light.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: GS2 and GOGAT1 are explicitly presented as components of the GS/GOGAT cycle, with GOGAT converting Gln to Glu downstream of GS activity.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results 2.1 states gln2 is slightly smaller, with leaf area ~70% of WT, and Results 2.3 shows increased vulnerability to high-light photoinhibition.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion broadly suggests nitrogen-fertilizer management and the value of nitrate assimilation for stress acclimation, but it does not specifically propose GS2 engineering as an applied target.

Omics_Data.reason: LC-HRMS profiling of 15N-labeled glutamine/glutamate is explicitly described; no public accession is reported in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly says GS/GOGAT participates in both de novo nitrate assimilation and photorespiration, and elevated CO2 data suggest the observed HL phenotype is not mainly due to photorespiration.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods 4.1 states glu1 is CS8611, At5g04140.

Applied_Species.reason: The experiments were conducted in Arabidopsis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly assess glutamine, glutamate, and chloroplastic glutathione redox potential/chl-EGSH.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Glutamine and glutamate are amino acids; chl-EGSH is a redox-state output associated with glutathione.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show glu1 has strongly reduced 15N incorporation, lower photosynthetic efficiency/ΦPSII, and lack of high-light chl-roGFP2 relaxation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present for glu1: mutant analysis, immunoblot, 15N LC-HRMS, chlorophyll fluorescence, chloroplast roGFP2, and elevated CO2 experiments.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These points match the Results: reduced GOGAT1 protein, lower 15N labeling, no significant light stimulation in glu1, chlorotic phenotype with lower PSII efficiency, and altered chl-roGFP2 relaxation pattern.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly refers to GOGAT1 as ferredoxin-dependent glutamate synthase 1 / Fd-GOGAT.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: GOGAT1 is discussed as Fd-dependent and as part of the chloroplastic GS/GOGAT cycle; Fd-GOGAT is presented in chloroplast-associated nitrogen assimilation/photosynthesis context.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The experiments used plants/leaves under various light conditions, but no direct expression pattern of GOGAT1 was measured or reported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These treatments are explicitly reported: NL 120, ML 800, HL 1200, dark, and elevated CO2 700/900 ppm.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaf tissue and chloroplast redox were measured, but explicit accumulation site for glutamine/glutamate is not stated.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper supports a role for nitrogen assimilation and chl-EGSH dynamics in redox homeostasis and photoprotection under high light.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Interaction with GS2 is supported at the pathway level, but direct protein-protein interaction is not shown; ferredoxin is functionally involved, yet not demonstrated as a direct interacting protein in this study.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results 2.1 and 2.3 show glu1 is small and chlorotic and has reduced ΦPSII even before/high-light treatment.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper discusses nitrate assimilation generally as beneficial under high light, but does not explicitly propose GOGAT1 engineering or breeding use as a tested conclusion.

Omics_Data.reason: LC-HRMS metabolite profiling of 15N-labeled glutamine and glutamate is explicitly described; no public accession is provided.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly mentions possible compensation by GOGAT2 or NADH-GOGAT and notes they are not the core tested targets.
